---
title: "Victoria transit - bus stop popularity for marketing"
sidebar: false
resources:
  - slides.pdf
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    page-layout: full
---

<div class="project-pdf-slides-root" hidden aria-hidden="true"></div>

:::: {.project-pdf-slides-intro}

McMaster research support for a Victoria, BC transit partner that needed **geographically meaningful** marketing priorities when **stop-level ridership** was not available for planning.

::::

## Project Overview

### Problem

The client wanted to know **which bus stops to prioritize** for marketing and outreach, but could not rely on measured boarding counts at each stop.

### Context

A citywide network of stops, finite campaign resources, and a need for segments that planners could **map and discuss**, not just a ranked spreadsheet.

### Goals

- Infer **relative demand profiles** at stops using features that *are* available (people, land use, destinations nearby).
- Produce **interpretable clusters** that marketing staff can turn into targeted campaigns.
- Keep claims honest: **inferred** demand as a staging tool, not a substitute for future ridership studies.

### Stakeholders

- **Transit operator** (Victoria): campaign planning and resource allocation.
- **McMaster** (research staff): methods design, analysis, and delivery captured in the PDF deck below.

### Final deliverable

A **slide deck (PDF)** presenting the approach, maps, and cluster interpretation-plus the workflow logic for reuse in other municipalities facing similar data gaps.

## Project Approach

### Scope & Assumptions

- **Scope (planned):** citywide Victoria stops; McMaster research support; **catchment-based** features; **unsupervised** grouping.
- **Assumption:** demographics, built form, and destination density will **proxy** latent demand well enough to **stage** campaigns (not to replace ridership studies later).

### Data Sources

- **Planned:** census demographics; land use; POIs; **walkable catchments** around each stop so stops stay comparable.

### Analytical Design

- **Plan:** assemble a **stop-level feature matrix** → **k-means** (or close variant) → **interpretable clusters** for messaging.
- Map clusters for planning conversations.

### Workflow / Process

**Planned steps:** define catchments → engineer features → cluster → **sanity-check** with the client → map segments for outreach.

### Constraints & Considerations

- I will flag **data currency** and **MAUP** effects around stops.
- The deck will state clearly: outputs are **inferred** demand, **not** measured ridership.

## Impact & Outcome

This project gave the client a practical way to prioritize marketing when direct ridership counts were unavailable. Instead of broad campaigns, they could focus outreach around stop clusters with the strongest inferred demand and strategic value. The workflow is also reusable for other municipalities facing similar data gaps.

:::: {.pdf-slides-embed-bar}
[Open PDF in new tab](slides.pdf){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary target="_blank" rel="noopener"}
::::

:::: {#pdf-slides-frame-wrap .pdf-slides-embed-full}
<iframe src="slides.pdf" title="Exploring the popularity of bus stops in Victoria, BC (PDF)" class="pdf-slides-embed"></iframe>
::::
